_comment: Lightweight PRD schema reference. This is the single source of truth for the structure that identity-mapper outputs and notion_writer.py consumes. Not a formal JSON Schema — just an annotated example.
api_version: string — e.g. 'REST API v3', 'Graph API 1.0', 'v2'. Use exact version string from official docs. If not explicitly stated: 'Data unavailable in public documentation'

sync_order._type: array of step objects — ordered sync execution plan with dependency rationale
entities: array of entity type strings to fetch in this step
sync_order.rationale: string — why these entities go in this step, what data dependencies exist

developer_summary: string — 3-4 sentences. Critical technical takeaways, potential bottlenecks, and gotchas the developer must keep in mind while building this integration. Include cross-cutting concerns like rate limit strategy, pagination traps, and NHI discovery approach.

data_source.spec_url: string or 'Data unavailable in public documentation'

overview.bottom_line: string — 1-2 sentence engineering-focused summary starting with 🎯 The Bottom Line:. What is the ONE thing an engineer must understand about this app's identity model?
overview.description: string — what the app does, framed from a security/identity perspective (not marketing)
overview.integration_purpose: string — why the platform integrates with this app. Structure as Read-Path (visibility) and Write-Path (remediation) objectives.

authentication.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: Which auth method to use and why, in one sentence.
authentication.method: string — OAuth 2.0 | API Key | Bearer | Basic Auth

rate_limits.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: The critical rate limit constraint and its production impact.
rate_limits.scope: string — per token | per user | per IP

pagination.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: How many strategies are needed and any hard limits to watch for.
pagination.strategy: string — cursor | offset | keyset | page-based | link-header. If multiple strategies exist for different endpoints, describe each as 'Strategy A: cursor (for X endpoints), Strategy B: offset (for Y endpoints)'

entity_accounts.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: What makes this app's account model unique for identity ingestion.

key_response_fields._type: dict — the platform field name → API field name string OR {api_field: string, notes: string} object. Use object form when a field needs critical engineering context (derivation logic, CRITICAL flags, caveats).

entity_accounts.human_vs_nonhuman: string — detection logic for this specific app. Include NHI mitigation strategy if direct detection is impossible (e.g., Audit Log synthesis).
architecture_warnings: array of strings — each a named warning with Action Required prescription. E.g., '🚨 Architecture Warning - The Dual API Inventory: Active users come from API A, deactivated from API B. Action Required: Merge both result sets, join on email.'

entity_groups.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: Key structural characteristic of groups in this app.

key_response_fields._type: dict — the platform field name → API field name string OR {api_field: string, notes: string} object.
key_response_fields.name: app field name

architecture_warnings: array of strings — named warnings about group structure, membership resolution, hierarchy, etc.

entity_roles.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: How this app models permissions and any traps.
entity_roles.list_endpoint: HTTP METHOD /path or 'Data unavailable in public documentation'

key_response_fields._type: dict — the platform field name → API field name string OR {api_field: string, notes: string} object.
key_response_fields.name: app field name

architecture_warnings: array of strings — warnings about admin flags outside role system, tier-dependent behavior, permission black boxes, etc.

entity_resources.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: What resources exist and how access is governed (ACL vs ownership vs permission-set-driven).

list_endpoints._type: dict — resource type → HTTP METHOD /path. Include Search API endpoints if resources are fetched via search.

entity_resources.access_list_endpoint: string — explicit ACL endpoint, or describe property-driven access model (e.g., 'Property-driven: read owner_id from resource object')

key_response_fields._type: dict — the platform field name → API field name string OR {api_field: string, notes: string} object. Use object form for owner_id with CRITICAL tag.
key_response_fields.name: app field name

architecture_warnings: array of strings — warnings about access model, search API pagination limits, ownership vs ACL patterns, etc.

entity_relations.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: How relationships are resolved in this app (embedded vs property-driven vs separate endpoints).
architecture_warnings: array of strings — warnings about N+1 queries, embedded data resolution, ownership model, etc.

remediation_actions.bottom_line: string — 🎯 The Bottom Line: Key architectural patterns for write-path actions (e.g., PUT full-replace vs PATCH partial-update).
architecture_warnings: array of strings — warnings about PUT vs PATCH semantics, indirect remediation paths, etc.

actions.action_name: string — e.g. Add User to Group
actions.notes: string — include 'Indirect — via [mechanism]' for workaround-based actions

technical_references.section: string — which PRD section this reference belongs to
technical_references.label: string — human-readable link text
technical_references.url: string — direct URL to official API documentation
